scenario: Rivendell Bank standardizes on the Java client across 4 backend teams to implement Camunda 8.8 job workers. A new developer asks which official client the Writing good workers page points to as the example for writing worker code in Java.
question: Which official client does the Camunda 8.8 Writing good workers page name for Java worker examples?

options.text: The official Camunda Java Client published under github.com/camunda/camunda-platform-get-started/tree/master/java, used as the example for writing job worker code in Java in the Writing good workers best practice

The Camunda 7 External Task Client for Java published under github.com/camunda/camunda-external-task-client-java, used as the worker library in the Writing good workers best practice page for Camunda 8.8

A community-maintained Java client published under github.com/camunda-community-hub; the official Camunda 8.8 Java client only ships as part of the Camunda Spring Boot Starter and not as a standalone library

A generated Java client built from the Zeebe gRPC proto files; the Writing good workers page steers Java developers to regenerate the client themselves rather than depend on a published Camunda Java Client artifact

a.text: Correct. The Java section of Writing good workers links to the Java Client at github.com/camunda/camunda-platform-get-started/tree/master/java and uses it as the example for writing worker code in Java.

b.text: Incorrect. The Camunda 7 External Task Client is a Camunda 7 artifact and is not the client linked from the Camunda 8.8 Writing good workers page.

c.text: Incorrect. The page points to the official camunda/camunda-platform-get-started Java repository, not a community fork, and the Java client is available standalone.

d.text: Incorrect. The best practice points developers at the published official Java client, not at regenerating one from gRPC proto files.

explanation: The Camunda 8.8 Writing good workers best practice names the official Java Client at github.com/camunda/camunda-platform-get-started/tree/master/java as the example library for Java worker code.
